mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate labor income, which drives consumption and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Nonfarm payrolls at 143915k in April 2016 confirm ongoing labor market expansion
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS increases, or rank correlation between PAYEMS change and 20-day returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.5% anchors short-term discount rates at historically low levels, forcing yield-seeking capital into equities and compressing the equity risk premium, which generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds rate at 0.37% in April 2016 remains near the zero lower bound
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce negative 60-day forward returns in more than 45% of observations, or mean 60-day return in triggered periods is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal broad-based demand acceleration across consumer and residential investment sectors, which outpaces supply constraints and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales at 453438M and housing starts at 1089k in early 2016 reflect steady demand
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year window, or an F-test for the interaction term fails to reject the null of zero incremental explanatory power (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market, industrial production growth signals rising capacity utilization and operating leverage, which expands profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; this relationship breaks when unemployment slack is elevated.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% in April 2016 indicates tight labor market conditions
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 6%, rising INDPRO coincides with negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of months over a 3-year window, or the spread between returns in tight versus slack regimes is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative 0.3 percentage point rise in the unemployment rate over a three-month window signals accelerating labor market deterioration that precedes downward earnings revisions and household balance-sheet stress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances over any 5-year rolling window, or the mean 60-day return during triggers is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a federal funds rate below 1% generate deeply negative real interest rates that reduce the opportunity cost of equity ownership and inflate risk asset valuations, jointly driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI at 237.92 with Fed funds at 0.37% in early 2016 produces negative real rates
failure_condition: The joint condition yields a rank correlation below 0.03 with 20-day forward returns over a 3-year window, or the interaction term between CPIAUCSL change and FEDFUNDS level adds no marginal predictive power (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects resilient household expenditure that translates into immediate revenue surprises for consumer-facing S&P 500 constituents, producing positive 5-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales at 453438M in April 2016 show continued consumer spending
failure_condition: Month-over-month RSAFS increases coincide with negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that spills over into corporate earnings guidance cuts and negative operating leverage, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production index at 103.3759 in March 2016 shows manufacturing softness
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines are followed by positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of episodes over a 5-year window, or the mean 60-day return following triggers is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).
